forbidden_meanings: A Euclidean metric circle in v0.
A set of planar points at fixed radius in v0.
entries.formal_definition: In v0, C_n is the finite cyclic address space modeled as ZMod n, with n > 0 for finite marked circles.
entries.intuition: A closed address space whose positions wrap around.
entries.name: Circle

forbidden_meanings: A geometric point in a plane unless a later geometry model is explicitly active.
entries.formal_definition: An element of C_n, represented by the residue class of i modulo n.
entries.intuition: A marked address on the circle.
entries.name: Node

forbidden_meanings: Physical spinning unless a later model interprets it physically.
entries.formal_definition: rot(n,k)(x) = x + k in C_n.
formal_models: addition by k in ZMod n
entries.intuition: Move k steps around the circle.
entries.name: Rotation

examples: scale(12,17)=scale(12,5) because 17 is congruent to 5 mod 12
scale(13,5) is invertible because 13 is prime and 1<=5<13
scale(12,4)(6)=0 as a second cofactor multiple
scale(12,4)(x)=0 exactly when period(12,4)=3 divides x
scale(12,4)(2)=scale(12,4)(5) exactly because 2 and 5 are congruent mod period(12,4)=3
on representatives 0,1,2 below period(12,4)=3, scale(12,4) is injective
scale_fiber_representative_set(12,4,2)=scale_fiber_representative_set(12,4,5) because 2 and 5 are congruent mod period(12,4)=3
natural x lies in scale_kernel_subgroup(12,4) exactly when 3 divides x
target 1 is outside scale_circle_image(12,4), so its target fiber is empty
target 8 is inside scale_circle_image(12,4), so its target fiber has card 4
scale_circle_image(12,4) has card 3 and the target 8 fiber has card 4, so 3*4=12
scale_circle_image(12,4) has card 3 and scale_kernel_representative_set(12,4) has card 4, so 3*4=12
scale(12,4)(2)=scale(12,4)(5) because 8 and 20 are congruent mod 12
scale(12,5)(2)=scale(12,5)(14) exactly because 2 and 14 are congruent mod 12
forbidden_meanings: A geometric dilation unless a later geometry model explicitly interprets it that way.
A proof of multiplication laws not linked to a theorem id.
entries.formal_definition: scale(n,k)(x) = k*x in C_n.
entries.intuition: Multiply every circle address by the same factor.
entries.name: Scaling
rewrite_rules: if k and l are congruent mod n, then scale(n,k)=scale(n,l)
scale(n,k) maps coil(n,stride,start) steps to coil(n,k*stride,k*start) steps
if p is prime and 1<=k<p, then scale(p,k) is bijective
if k divides n, then scale(n,k)(n/k)=0 in C_n
if k divides n, then scale(n,k)(m*(n/k))=0 in C_n
if k divides n, then scale(n,k)(x+m*(n/k))=scale(n,k)(x)
scale(n,k)(x)=0 iff n divides k*x for natural representatives
for n != 0, scale(n,k)(x)=0 iff period(n,k) divides x for natural representatives
scale(n,k)(x)=scale(n,k)(y) iff k*x and k*y are congruent mod n for natural representatives
for n != 0, scale(n,k)(x)=scale(n,k)(y) iff x and y are congruent mod period(n,k)
for n != 0 and x,y < period(n,k), scale(n,k)(x)=scale(n,k)(y) iff x=y
for n != 0, scale(n,k)(x) belongs to scale_period_representative_image(n,k)
for n != 0 and r < n, scale_fiber_representative_set(n,k,r)=scale_fiber_representative_set(n,k,s) iff r and s are congruent mod period(n,k)
for n != 0, natural representative x lies in scale_kernel_subgroup(n,k) iff period(n,k) divides x
if target is not in scale_circle_image(n,k), then scale_target_fiber_representative_set(n,k,target) is empty
for n != 0 and target in scale_circle_image(n,k), card(scale_target_fiber_representative_set(n,k,target))=gcd(n,k)
for n != 0 and target in scale_circle_image(n,k), card(scale_circle_image(n,k))*card(scale_target_fiber_representative_set(n,k,target))=n
if gcd(n,k)=1, then scale(n,k)(x)=scale(n,k)(y) iff x and y are congruent mod n

forbidden_meanings: A visual spiral without a formal orbit rule.
entries.formal_definition: The orbit {start + t*k mod n | t in Nat}.
entries.intuition: The path traced by repeatedly applying the same rotation.
entries.name: Coil

examples: stride 4 on C_12 closes after 3 steps
entries.formal_definition: A coil closes at step m when start + m*k = start in C_n.
formal_models: zero multiple condition in ZMod n
entries.intuition: The repeated motion has returned to its start address.
entries.name: Closure
rewrite_rules: start + m*k = start iff m*k = 0 in C_n

forbidden_meanings: The length of a drawn curve.
entries.formal_definition: The least positive m such that m*k = 0 in C_n; in Lean v0, addOrderOf (k : ZMod n).
entries.intuition: How many steps before the coil returns.
entries.name: Period

examples: stride 5 on C_13 is a full coil
entries.formal_definition: A stride-k coil is full when its period is n, equivalently when it visits every node before returning.
formal_models: generator of a cyclic additive group
entries.intuition: One repeated stride covers the whole circle.
entries.name: Full Coil

examples: stride 4 on C_12 has four orbit classes
forbidden_meanings: A connected component of a topological space.
A visual cycle without the generated-subgroup equivalence relation.
entries.formal_definition: In v0, a stride-k orbit class is a coset of the additive subgroup generated by k in C_n.
formal_models: quotient of ZMod n by AddSubgroup.zmultiples (k : ZMod n)
entries.intuition: One of the disjoint cycles produced by repeatedly moving by the same stride.
entries.name: Orbit Class
rewrite_rules: two nodes lie in the same orbit class iff their difference is an integer multiple of the stride in C_n

forbidden_meanings: The number of rendered arrows in a diagram.
entries.formal_definition: The number of cosets of the additive subgroup generated by k in C_n.
entries.intuition: How many disjoint stride cycles the circle splits into.
entries.name: Orbit Class Count

forbidden_meanings: A continuous spiral coordinate in v0.
entries.formal_definition: A lifted node records q full rotations and residue r, representing q*n+r.
entries.intuition: Counting without losing full-turn information modulo n.
entries.name: Winding

forbidden_meanings: A physical jump between geometric points without the quotient/remainder rule.
entries.formal_definition: The lifted successor operation increments the residue by one and carries into the winding when the residue crosses the modulus.
entries.intuition: One more step around the finite circle, preserving full-turn count.
entries.name: Successor With Carry

forbidden_meanings: Vector addition in a geometric plane.
entries.formal_definition: Lifted addition concatenates two natural path counts by adding their winding components, adding their residues, and carrying residue overflow into the winding count.
formal_models: natural-number addition expressed through quotient/remainder arithmetic
entries.intuition: Walk one lifted path, then keep walking the second; full turns and leftover address combine with carry.
entries.name: Lifted Addition

entries.formal_definition: Repeated application of the lifted successor transition to a valid winding/residue state.
formal_models: primitive recursion over Nat using quotient/remainder successor
entries.intuition: Induction as taking one unit step at a time around the lifted circle.
entries.name: Iterated Lifted Successor

entries.formal_definition: The source-of-truth metadata file for theorem ids, Lean names, proof status, paper references, dictionary dependencies, and verification requirements.
entries.intuition: The ledger that prevents papers from outrunning proofs.
entries.name: Theorem Manifest

forbidden_meanings: An informal appendix with no proof checker.
entries.formal_definition: A per-paper directory containing Lean files that state, check, or re-export the formal declarations cited by that paper.
entries.intuition: The machine-checkable companion to a paper.
entries.name: Proof Sidecar

entries.formal_definition: A per-paper directory containing executable examples, tests, and diagram-generation code that supports but does not prove the paper's claims.
entries.intuition: The runnable intuition layer beside the proof layer.
entries.name: Python Sidecar

examples: Godel coil numbering must be treated as arithmetization, not escape.
forbidden_meanings: A proof that Circle Calculus is complete for all arithmetic truth.
entries.formal_definition: The invalid claim that a new encoding avoids incompleteness merely by changing notation or representation.
entries.intuition: Circle encodings can arithmetize syntax, but they do not evade Godel boundaries.
entries.name: Godel Escape Fallacy

entries.formal_definition: A finite list whose i-th entry counts i-dimensional cells in a combinatorial model.
entries.intuition: The bookkeeping vector behind Euler characteristic and suspension formulas.
entries.name: Finite cell-count list

entries.formal_definition: The alternating sum of a finite cell-count list.
entries.intuition: A dimension-sensitive count invariant for finite combinatorial models.
entries.name: Euler characteristic

forbidden_meanings: A continuous suspension proof without a separate formal model.
A picture of a suspension treated as a proof.
entries.formal_definition: The finite suspension count transform that adds two poles and cones each cell dimension to the next dimension.
entries.intuition: The finite combinatorial move used to climb the sphere ladder.
entries.name: Suspension cell-count transform

forbidden_meanings: A proof of a general stable homotopy equivalence.
A claim that all stable invariants are Euler characteristic.
entries.formal_definition: The operation that applies the finite suspension cell-count transform twice, preserving Euler characteristic in the finite-count model.
entries.intuition: The smallest stable-sphere seed in the current finite model: two suspension moves return the Euler characteristic to its starting value.
entries.name: Double suspension Euler stabilizer

forbidden_meanings: A statement about spectra without a separate formal model.
entries.formal_definition: The operation that applies the finite double-suspension Euler stabilizer twice.
entries.intuition: A first iterated stable-clock check: two double-suspension steps still preserve the finite Euler characteristic.
entries.name: Four suspension Euler stabilizer

forbidden_meanings: A proof about real Clifford algebras.
entries.formal_definition: The finite clock index dimension % 8 used as a bookkeeping layer for future Bott and Clifford periodicity work.
entries.intuition: A safe way to talk about an eight-step dimension cycle before formalizing Clifford algebras or Bott periodicity.
entries.name: Period-eight dimension clock

forbidden_meanings: A proof that every bundle is globally trivial.
entries.formal_definition: A product object with a visible base coordinate and a fiber coordinate, modeled in Lean by Circle.Phase2.TrivialBundle.
entries.intuition: The safe first bundle model: base and hidden fiber are tracked separately, without claiming any twisting.
entries.name: Trivial product bundle

forbidden_meanings: A projection from an arbitrary bundle without a separate formal model.
entries.formal_definition: The map that returns the base coordinate from the trivial product-bundle seed.
entries.intuition: The visible part of a base/fiber state.
entries.name: Bundle projection

forbidden_meanings: A connection, curvature, holonomy, or transition-function model.
entries.formal_definition: The map that returns the fiber coordinate from the trivial product-bundle seed.
entries.intuition: The hidden or phase-like part of a base/fiber state in the product model.
entries.name: Fiber coordinate

entries.formal_definition: A finite one-dimensional oriented interval with integer source and target coordinates.
entries.intuition: The smallest boundary-calculus object: an oriented segment whose boundary is target minus source.
entries.name: Directed interval

forbidden_meanings: A full singular, cellular, or simplicial boundary operator.
entries.formal_definition: In the directed-interval seed, intervalBoundary maps an interval to target minus source, and pointBoundary maps zero-dimensional boundary chains to zero.
entries.intuition: A tiny checked model of the boundary-after-boundary-is-zero slogan.
entries.name: Boundary operator seed

entries.formal_definition: The operation that swaps the source and target of a directed interval.
entries.intuition: Turning an oriented segment around reverses the sign of its boundary.
entries.name: Interval orientation reversal

forbidden_meanings: A rendered diagram with no theorem id.
entries.formal_definition: A certificate record carrying a glyph id, theorem id, and Lean declaration name.
entries.intuition: The smallest proof-carrying diagram contract: a visual object must point to formal identifiers.
entries.name: Proof glyph certificate

forbidden_meanings: A proof that the theorem id is semantically correct.
entries.formal_definition: The projection that reads the theorem id carried by a proof glyph certificate.
entries.intuition: The paper can ask which theorem a glyph claims to represent.
entries.name: Glyph theorem-id projection

forbidden_meanings: A proof that the Lean declaration proves the intended semantic diagram.
entries.formal_definition: The projection that reads the Lean declaration name carried by a proof glyph certificate.
entries.intuition: The paper can ask which checked Lean declaration a glyph points to.
entries.name: Glyph Lean-name projection

forbidden_meanings: A discovered period in real data.
entries.formal_definition: The residue step % period for a positive finite period.
entries.intuition: The simplest coordinate for synthetic periodic data: where a sample lies inside its current cycle.
entries.name: Finite phase coordinate

forbidden_meanings: A claim that an arbitrary dataset has this period.
entries.formal_definition: The finite phase coordinate is unchanged after adding one positive period.
entries.intuition: One full cycle returns a periodic coordinate to the same phase.
entries.name: Phase closure

forbidden_meanings: A memory-safety proof for an implementation language.
entries.formal_definition: The finite wraparound address index % size for a positive circular buffer size.
entries.intuition: The proof-carrying address rewrite behind ring buffers, circular queues, and cyclic kernels.
entries.name: Cyclic address

forbidden_meanings: A proof that a backend is faster.
A proof that arbitrary strided memory is coalesced.
entries.formal_definition: The cyclic address is unchanged after adding one positive circular buffer size.
entries.intuition: A legal rewrite for circular computation: one full wrap returns to the same address.
entries.name: Cyclic address wraparound

entries.formal_definition: The finite schedule sample % bin_count for assigning samples or rays to direction bins.
entries.intuition: A minimal checked address for ray queues, hemisphere samples, and spherical-coil buckets.
entries.name: Direction-bin schedule

forbidden_meanings: A proof that binning improves ray coherence.
A statement about noise quality or visual convergence.
entries.formal_definition: The direction-bin schedule is unchanged after adding one positive bin count.
entries.intuition: A finite queue/sampler schedule repeats after one full pass through the bins.
entries.name: Direction-bin closure

forbidden_meanings: A proof of FFT or NTT correctness.
A claim that a layout is faster.
entries.formal_definition: The finite address (step * stride) % size for a circular stride schedule.
entries.intuition: The address pattern behind orbit-order layout, periodic stencils, and finite transform passes.
entries.name: Stride address

entries.formal_definition: The finite stride address is unchanged after adding one full circular size to the step count.
entries.intuition: A stride schedule repeats after one full pass through the circular step horizon.
entries.name: Stride schedule closure

entries.formal_definition: The finite schedule tick % slot_count for a positive number of scheduler slots.
entries.intuition: A systems-facing cyclic schedule primitive for runtimes, queues, timers, and load rotation.
entries.name: Round-robin slot

entries.formal_definition: The round-robin slot is unchanged after adding one positive slot count.
entries.intuition: After a full pass through all slots, the schedule returns to the same slot.
entries.name: Round-robin closure

forbidden_meanings: A proof of better model quality.
entries.formal_definition: The finite phase channel position % period for a positive AI phase period.
entries.intuition: A simple periodic feature-index primitive for Circle AI architecture experiments.
entries.name: AI phase channel

forbidden_meanings: A claim that circles improve all AI.
entries.formal_definition: The AI phase channel is unchanged after adding one positive phase period.
entries.intuition: A periodic feature-index returns to the same phase after one full period.
entries.name: AI phase-channel closure

forbidden_meanings: A proof that cyclic memory replaces attention.
entries.formal_definition: The finite memory-bank slot token % bank_size for a positive bank size.
entries.intuition: A CoilKV-style cyclic address for memory-bank experiments.
entries.name: Cyclic memory slot

entries.formal_definition: The cyclic memory slot is unchanged after adding one positive bank size.
entries.intuition: A token returns to the same memory slot after one full pass through a cyclic bank.
entries.name: Cyclic memory-slot closure

entries.formal_definition: The finite adapter block channel % block_size for a positive block size.
entries.intuition: A block/cyclic channel index for CoilRA and block-structured adapter experiments.
entries.name: Adapter block index

entries.formal_definition: The adapter block index is unchanged after adding one positive block size.
entries.intuition: A block-structured adapter index repeats after one full pass through the block.
entries.name: Adapter block closure

forbidden_meanings: A proof that the Lean declaration has the intended semantics.
entries.formal_definition: A finite metadata record carrying a theorem id and Lean declaration name, modeled by `Circle.Phase2.TheoremMetadata`.
entries.intuition: The smallest source-of-truth object a proof glyph can resolve against.
entries.name: Theorem metadata entry

entries.formal_definition: `Circle.Phase2.proofGlyphValidAgainst`, requiring a proof glyph's theorem id and Lean declaration name to match some theorem metadata entry.
entries.intuition: A proof-carrying glyph must resolve to tracked theorem metadata before readers trust the interface.
entries.name: Glyph manifest validity

forbidden_meanings: A proof of Hopf fibration transition functions.
entries.formal_definition: `Circle.Phase2.BundleTransition`, a base-preserving fiber map over the trivial bundle seed.
entries.intuition: The first checked transition-function boundary: fiber data may move while the visible base coordinate stays fixed.
entries.name: Bundle transition seed

forbidden_meanings: A connection, curvature, or holonomy theorem.
entries.formal_definition: `Circle.Phase2.bundleTransitionCompose`, composing two base-preserving fiber maps in the trivial bundle seed.
entries.intuition: A finite sanity law for future overlap/cocycle work.
entries.name: Bundle transition composition

forbidden_meanings: Evidence that coil signatures outperform baselines.
entries.formal_definition: `circle_math.applications.coil_data.benchmark_known_period`, a deterministic fixture with known-period synthetic signals and candidate period rankings.
entries.intuition: A small benchmark harness for testing coil-period ideas before touching real data.
entries.name: Synthetic period benchmark

forbidden_meanings: A formal proof of hidden periodicity.
entries.formal_definition: `circle_math.applications.coil_data.coil_closure_error`, the mean squared difference between a signal and itself shifted by a candidate period.
entries.intuition: A simple closure profile score: lower error means the candidate period closes the sampled coil better.
entries.name: Coil closure error

forbidden_meanings: A proof that Fourier or periodogram methods are unnecessary.
entries.formal_definition: `circle_math.applications.coil_data.autocorrelation_score`, the normalized autocorrelation score for a candidate lag.
entries.intuition: The ordinary baseline the coil closure profile must be compared against before usefulness claims.
entries.name: Autocorrelation period baseline

entries.formal_definition: The finite S^0 model consisting of exactly two opposed points, represented in Lean by Circle.S0.Sign.
entries.intuition: The smallest sphere-like opposition pair.
entries.name: Two-point opposition

forbidden_meanings: A continuous sphere map without a finite model.
entries.formal_definition: The finite map Circle.S0.antipode that swaps the two points of S^0.
entries.intuition: Opposition as an involutive flip.
entries.name: S0 antipode

forbidden_meanings: Identifying one point with two opposed points.
entries.formal_definition: C_1 is a one-node finite cyclic address object; S^0 is a two-point opposition object.
entries.intuition: The topological base sphere and the degenerate one-node circle are different objects.
entries.name: C1 is not S0 warning

forbidden_meanings: A continuous metric circle unless a later model explicitly activates that interpretation.
entries.formal_definition: The active finite circle core modeled by C_n, rotations, coils, period, orbit decomposition, and lifted winding.
entries.intuition: The dimension-one foundation from which higher-dimensional finite models inherit cyclic behavior.
entries.name: S1 finite circle core

forbidden_meanings: A new circle object separate from C_n.
entries.formal_definition: `Circle.S1.signedRot n k x = x + (k : ZMod n)` for integer stride `k`.
entries.intuition: Clockwise and counterclockwise motion are the same finite circle operation with signed stride.
entries.name: Signed rotation

forbidden_meanings: A proof of all integer arithmetic by itself.
entries.formal_definition: `Circle.S1.signedRot_inverse` proves that moving by `k` and then by `-k` returns to the starting node.
entries.intuition: Orientation adds reversibility to the finite-circle motion model.
entries.name: Reversible signed motion

forbidden_meanings: A completed proof of the full octonionic Hopf fibration or quotient topology.
entries.formal_definition: The roadmap target recorded by `Circle.Future.S15.octonionicHopfRoadmap` for the future structure S^7 -> S^15 -> S^8.
entries.intuition: The post-S7 horizon now anchored by bounded topological and coordinate Hopf facts.
entries.name: Future octonionic Hopf horizon

forbidden_meanings: A smooth or analytic model of S15.
entries.formal_definition: `Circle.Future.S15.topologicalModel`, obtained by suspending the finite S7 model eight more times, with Euler characteristic proved by `Circle.Future.S15.topologicalModel_eulerCharacteristic`.
entries.intuition: A bounded topological anchor for the S15 horizon.
entries.name: Finite S15 suspension model

forbidden_meanings: A proof of octonionic phase quotient, global bundle structure, or S7 fiber action.
entries.formal_definition: `Circle.Future.S15.octonionicHopf_lands_sphere`, proving that normalized octonion pairs land on the unit nine-coordinate base equation under the bounded coordinate Hopf map.
entries.intuition: The coordinate landing equation for the octonionic Hopf horizon before full fibration topology.
entries.name: Bounded octonionic Hopf landing

forbidden_meanings: A completed proof that no valid octonionic Hopf fibration exists.
Permission to ignore S7O-W0002 bracketing requirements.
entries.formal_definition: `Circle.Future.S15.octonionicHopfMap_rightPhase_not_invariant_example` proves that shared right multiplication by a unit octonion does not preserve the full bounded octonionic Hopf coordinate in the current bracketing.
entries.intuition: The octonionic horizon needs bracketed quotient/fiber work; copying the S3 or quaternionic S7 phase story is not sound.
entries.name: Naive octonionic phase invariance warning

forbidden_meanings: A drawing treated as a proof.
entries.formal_definition: A finite S^2 count model formed by suspending a finite circle C_n with north and south poles.
entries.intuition: The first sphere surface obtained by coning a finite circle from two poles.
entries.name: Suspended finite circle

forbidden_meanings: A Cartesian product C_n x C_m.
entries.formal_definition: A finite S^2 grid model with two collapsed poles and r latitude rings, each with n cyclic addresses.
entries.intuition: Latitude rings carry S1 behavior while poles handle sphere collapse.
entries.name: Sphere grid

forbidden_meanings: A longitude coordinate at a pole.
entries.formal_definition: A non-pole ring inside SphereGrid(n,r), modeled as C_n in the finite latitude-ring theorem.
entries.intuition: A horizontal cyclic slice of the finite sphere grid.
entries.name: Latitude ring

forbidden_meanings: A second cyclic coordinate at the poles.
entries.formal_definition: The relation that identifies all north cone tips as one pole and all south cone tips as one pole in the finite sphere model.
entries.intuition: The mechanism that makes a sphere grid sphere-like rather than torus-like.
entries.name: Pole collapse

forbidden_meanings: A full continuous antipodal map on every sphere model.
entries.formal_definition: `Circle.S2.suspendedCircleAntipode` swaps the poles of a suspended finite circle and negates the equator coordinate.
entries.intuition: Opposite surface points in the finite suspended-circle model.
entries.name: Suspended-circle antipode

forbidden_meanings: A chosen longitude at a collapsed pole.
entries.formal_definition: The north/south pole pair swapped by `Circle.S2.suspendedCircleAntipode_swapsPoles`.
entries.intuition: The simplest axis structure in the finite suspended sphere.
entries.name: Pole axis

forbidden_meanings: Calling a product of circles a sphere.
entries.formal_definition: C_n x C_m has two cyclic directions and is torus-like, not sphere-like.
entries.intuition: A finite sphere grid requires pole collapse.
entries.name: Torus confusion warning

forbidden_meanings: Assigning independent longitude addresses to a collapsed pole.
entries.formal_definition: Longitude is not a meaningful coordinate at a collapsed pole.
entries.intuition: All longitudes meet at each pole.
entries.name: Pole singularity warning

forbidden_meanings: Using group APIs for S^2 points without a separate proven structure.
entries.formal_definition: S^2 does not have the same natural pointwise multiplication structure as S^1 or unit-quaternion S^3.
entries.intuition: The sphere surface is geometric here, not a cyclic or quaternionic group.
entries.name: S2 group fallacy warning

forbidden_meanings: A visual 4D projection treated as a proof.
entries.formal_definition: The finite S^3 suspension count model `Circle.S3.suspendedSuspendedCircleCounts`, with Euler characteristic proved by `Circle.S3.suspendedSuspendedCircle_chi`.
entries.intuition: The combinatorial topological S^3 layer before quaternion algebra.
entries.name: Finite 3-sphere suspension model

forbidden_meanings: A proof that S^3 is globally S^2 x S^1.
entries.formal_definition: The coordinate map `Circle.S3.hopfMap : Circle.S3.HopfPair -> Circle.S3.HopfBasePoint`, with `Circle.S3.hopfMap_lands_sphere` proving that normalized pairs land on the unit base equation.
entries.intuition: A visible S^2 base with hidden S^1 phase fibers.
entries.name: Hopf map

entries.formal_definition: The common unit-phase action `Circle.S3.phaseRotatePair`; `Circle.S3.hopfMap_phase_invariant` proves it preserves Hopf base coordinates, and `Circle.S3.hopfFiber_circle_like` proves it also preserves normalized Hopf pairs.
entries.intuition: The hidden circle above each visible base point.
entries.name: Hopf phase fiber

forbidden_meanings: Declaring S^3 equal to S^2 x S^1.
entries.formal_definition: The Hopf fibration is locally product-like but S^3 is not globally S^2 x S^1.
entries.intuition: Hidden phase is twisted globally.
entries.name: Hopf not product warning

entries.formal_definition: The algebraic S^3 track using real quaternions `Circle.S3.RealQuaternion = Quaternion ℝ` and the norm-square map `Circle.S3.quaternionNorm`.
entries.intuition: S^3 as the first higher sphere with native associative but noncommutative multiplication.
entries.name: Quaternion model

entries.formal_definition: A real quaternion bundled with a proof that `Circle.S3.quaternionNorm q = 1`.
entries.intuition: A point of the algebraic S^3 model.
entries.name: Unit quaternion

entries.formal_definition: The formal example `Circle.S3.quaternion_noncommutative_example`, proving that `i*j != j*i` in integer quaternions.
entries.intuition: S^3: order matters.
entries.name: Quaternion noncommutativity

forbidden_meanings: A complete SO(3) formalization by itself.
entries.formal_definition: The real-quaternion action `Circle.S3.quaternionConjugationAction q v = q * v * star q`.
entries.intuition: The algebraic core of rotating by conjugation before quotienting signs.
entries.name: Quaternion conjugation action

entries.formal_definition: `q` and `-q` give the same value under `Circle.S3.quaternionConjugationAction`.
entries.intuition: The first formal shadow of the spin double cover: opposite unit quaternions act the same.
entries.name: Quaternion sign ambiguity

forbidden_meanings: A proved quaternionic Hopf formalization by itself.
entries.formal_definition: The finite S4 count model `Circle.S4.counts`, with Euler characteristic proved by `Circle.S4.eulerCharacteristic`, kept visible as the future base of S^3 -> S^7 -> S^4.
entries.intuition: The geometric base needed before quaternionic Hopf claims.
entries.name: S4 quaternionic Hopf base

forbidden_meanings: A claim about every smooth structure on every sphere.
entries.formal_definition: The finite-suspension result represented by `Circle.S4.iteratedSuspensionEulerParity`: the S4/S5/S6 iterated suspension counts have Euler characteristics 2, 0, and 2.
entries.intuition: The bridge that carries S^4, S^5, and S^6 without skipping dimensions.
entries.name: Iterated suspension Euler parity

entries.formal_definition: The finite S5 count model `Circle.S5.counts`, with Euler characteristic proved by `Circle.S5.eulerCharacteristic`, kept as a bridge toward future projective and circle-bundle structures.
entries.intuition: A higher odd sphere that remains part of the geometric ladder.
entries.name: S5 complex projective bridge

entries.formal_definition: The finite S6 count model `Circle.S6.counts`, with Euler characteristic proved by `Circle.S6.eulerCharacteristic`, kept as the warning layer near octonionic geometry.
entries.intuition: A geometric warning layer before S^7 octonions.
entries.name: S6 octonion shadow

forbidden_meanings: Settling the S^6 complex-structure problem by roadmap language.
entries.formal_definition: Claims about integrable complex structures on S^6 are not project assumptions unless formalized from accepted foundations.
entries.intuition: Do not build on delicate unresolved S^6 claims.
entries.name: S6 complex-structure warning

entries.formal_definition: The finite S7 model `Circle.S7.iteratedSuspensionModel`, built by suspending `Circle.S6.counts` and proved to have Euler characteristic 0 by `Circle.S7.eulerCharacteristic`.
entries.intuition: The topological S^7 layer before octonion algebra.
entries.name: Topological S7 suspension model

entries.formal_definition: The bounded Cayley-Dickson coordinate model `Circle.S7.OctonionCoord`, with Lean proofs for basis unit norm, conjugate norm, norm multiplicativity, unit closure by norm, noncommutativity, and nonassociativity.
entries.intuition: S^7: order and grouping matter.
entries.name: Octonion model

forbidden_meanings: An element of a group under ordinary octonion multiplication.
entries.formal_definition: An octonion with squared norm one in the coordinate model; `Circle.S7.unitOctonion_mul_closed` proves products of norm-one octonions remain norm-one, without implying associativity or a group API.
entries.intuition: A unit element of the nonassociative octonion layer.
entries.name: Unit octonion

forbidden_meanings: Using group APIs without a separate formal justification.
entries.formal_definition: Unit octonions are nonassociative under ordinary multiplication and must not be treated as a group.
entries.intuition: The S^7 algebraic jump changes the proof rules.
entries.name: Unit octonions are not a group warning

entries.formal_definition: In octonion calculus, operation grouping is part of the statement because multiplication is nonassociative.
entries.intuition: Parentheses matter.
entries.name: Octonion bracketing warning

forbidden_meanings: A completed proof before S3 and S4 foundations are stable.
entries.formal_definition: The coordinate quaternionic Hopf model `Circle.S7.quaternionicHopfMap`, with `Circle.S7.quaternionicHopf_lands_sphere` proving that normalized quaternion pairs land on the unit five-coordinate base equation and `Circle.S7.quaternionicPhaseInvariance` proving right unit-quaternion phase invariance.
entries.intuition: The higher hidden-phase analog of Hopf coils.
entries.name: Quaternionic Hopf roadmap

forbidden_meanings: A rhetorical claim that substitutes for formal verification.
entries.formal_definition: A theorem must not be described as proved unless its manifest status and Lean declaration support that claim.
entries.intuition: The papers are allowed to be ambitious, but proof status must be exact.
entries.name: Proof status honesty

forbidden_meanings: Treating a projection or diagram as a proof object.
entries.formal_definition: Diagrams, projections, and rendered examples explain a construction but do not prove it.
entries.intuition: Visual artifacts support papers; Lean carries formal proof status.
entries.name: Pictures are not proofs
